text: Clear width of an accessible doorway shall be at least 32 inches (0.815m) measured between the face of the door and the opposite stop, with the door open 90 degrees.
Maneuvering clearance on the latch side of a doorway shall be at least 18 inches beyond the latch side of the door, in addition to the door width, on the pull side.
The minimum clear width for a single wheelchair accessible route shall be 36 inches (0.915m) except at doors, where 32 inches minimum clear width is permitted for a maximum depth of 24 inches.
The least possible slope for a ramp on an accessible route shall be used. The maximum slope of a ramp shall be 1:12. The maximum rise for any run shall be 30 inches.
Every accessible ramp with a rise greater than 6 inches shall have handrails on both sides. Handrail height shall be 34 to 38 inches above ramp surface.

Any stairway that is provided as a required means of egress shall have an accessible route (ramp or elevator) available as an alternative for wheelchair and mobility-device users, unless an exception applies.
Elevators serving as part of an accessible route shall have a minimum clear floor area sufficient for wheelchair turning and shall be located on an accessible route from an accessible entrance.
Where the accessible route makes a 180-degree turn around an obstruction less than 48 inches wide, clear width shall be 42 inches minimum at the turn.
The clear width of an exit access corridor shall not be less than 44 inches, except that corridors serving fewer than 50 occupants may be 36 inches.
The minimum width of a means of egress door shall provide a clear width of not less than 32 inches, and doors shall be capable of opening at least 90 degrees.
Exit access travel distance shall not exceed 250 feet in a building not equipped with an automatic sprinkler system, and shall not exceed 300 feet where sprinklered.
Every space occupied shall have access to at least two independent exits, unless the space qualifies for a single exit exception based on occupant load and travel distance.
